commit.message:
Simplify auth: locationId + Anthropic API key replaces hlpt_ key system

- authenticate.js: accept x-location-id, validate Anthropic key stored
- POST /api/activate: public endpoint to store Anthropic key (first-time setup)
- AppContext: use locationId from URL, activate() flow replaces login()
- AuthGate: ask for Anthropic API key instead of hlpt_ key
- toolRegistry: export saveToolConfig and loadToolConfigs
- /claude/status: async, reads claudeReady from stored configs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add iteration example to README showing multi-call workflow

Shows how calling /auto-research repeatedly accumulates state,
with example output for 3 iterations including NEW BEST and REGRESSED.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: wire 5 write-only systems into execution path

KsetraJna: mid-turn observation in engine loop, is_stuck() injects
  stagnation guidance — was observe-only at turn boundary.
Cetana: anomaly flag bridges heartbeat → engine, health warnings
  injected mid-turn — was emitting into void SignalBus.
VenuOrchestrator: DIW position modulates cache TTL (15-60s) — was
  stepping a counter that went nowhere.
SVC_COMPRESSION: engine uses singleton from registry — was creating
  its own instance, bypassing the registered service.
SVC_FEEDBACK: tool outcomes feed signal_success/signal_failure — was
  registered but never consumed.
SVC_NORTH_STAR: alignment check at task start via XOR distance — was
  computed at boot and never read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ui): expose typed field names on form() for compile-time validation (#1051) (#1098)

Add a `fields` property to the form instance that provides typed field
names derived from the schema type parameter. This enables compile-time
safety for input `name` attributes — typos and schema renames are caught
by TypeScript instead of at runtime.

- Add `FieldNames<TBody>` mapped type: `{ readonly [K in keyof TBody & string]: K }`
- Add `fields` to `FormBaseProperties<TBody>` and `ReservedFormNames`
- Runtime implementation uses a Proxy that returns the property key as-is
- Type-level tests verify: positive (literal types), negative (nonExistent, wrong literal)
- Runtime tests verify: field name returns correct string, object identity stability

Closes #1051

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix billing date source of truth and restore missing billing dates

**Problem**: System had two conflicting sources for renewal dates:
- Database: accounts.next_billing_date (used by cron job)
- UI: Calculated from members.join_date (displayed on admin page)

This caused 49 accounts to show renewal dates in UI but have NULL in database,
preventing them from being billed by the cron job.

**Changes**:
1. Updated admin/members page to use accounts.next_billing_date (single source of truth)
   - Removed getNextRenewal() function that calculated from join_date
   - Added getNextBillingDate() that reads from accounts.next_billing_date
   - Updated Member interface to include next_billing_date from accounts
   - Updated database query to fetch next_billing_date

2. Fixed HOWTO.md schema documentation
   - Changed next_renewal_date → next_billing_date
   - Added note: "Source of truth for when account should be billed next (used by cron job)"

**Impact**:
- Admin members page now shows accurate billing dates from database
- UI and cron job now use same source of truth (accounts.next_billing_date)
- Billing dates restored for 49 accounts based on subscription_start_date
- 3 accounts with billing day 9 set to today (March 9) for immediate billing

**Files Modified**:
- src/pages/admin/members.tsx - Use database column instead of calculated value
- HOWTO.md - Fix schema documentation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- checkpoints/ (created by experiments)

commit.message:
fix: correct JS runtime detection and improve yt-dlp error messages

- Use 'node' not 'nodejs' for --js-runtimes (yt-dlp only accepts
  deno, node, bun, quickjs)
- Remove --extractor-args mweb override that made downloads worse
  (mweb requires PO token, default clients work better)
- Add _friendly_ytdlp_error() to convert raw yt-dlp stderr into
  actionable messages for bot detection, missing runtime, and
  format unavailable errors
- Production servers need Node.js or Deno installed for YouTube
  downloads (YouTube deprecated runtime-less extraction)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to Supabase + Flutter architecture

- Replace broken Flask/SQLite monolith (rover.py) with Supabase:
  PostgreSQL schema with PostGIS, Row Level Security, and Edge Functions
- Add supabase/schema.sql: all tables (profiles, events, event_subscriptions,
  pickup_requests), indexes, and RLS policies per role
- Add schedule-pickup Edge Function: greedy nearest-neighbor route ordering
  with ETA calculation and FCM notification for first user in queue
- Add send-notification Edge Function: real FCM push (replaces print())
- Wire Flutter frontend to Supabase via supabase_flutter SDK:
  AuthService, EventService, PickupService
- Add screens: RegisterPage, UserHomePage, DriverHomePage, AdminHomePage,
  EventDetailPage with realtime pickup status via Supabase streams
- Update main.dart: Supabase + Firebase init, AuthGate session check,
  login button wired to AuthService, signup navigates to RegisterPage
- Update pubspec.yaml: supabase_flutter, firebase_core, firebase_messaging,
  geolocator; add OpenSans font declaration
- Update .gitignore: exclude .env and Supabase temp dirs
- Add .env.example: safe-to-commit secrets template

Fixes all 7 critical bugs and 5 security vulnerabilities from audit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Rover
repository.description: This Flask-based application simplifies event pickup management, driver assignments, and user registrations. Ideal for coordinating transportation logistics, it offers a user-friendly interface for event organizers, drivers, and attendees.

commit.message:
Handle tournaments with standings-less final rounds

Some tournaments (e.g. RC finals) have a final playoff round that
contains match results but no standings endpoint. The fetch script now
walks backwards from the last completed round to find standings, then
uses applyStandingsLessRoundAdjustments() to infer final rankings from
match outcomes. Also re-fetches 392336 with no-show cleaning applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

install: fix polkit path bug, add arch check, auto-enable extension

- Always install batctl to /usr/local/bin/batctl so the polkit exec.path
  always matches — previously, if batctl was already in PATH elsewhere
  (e.g. /usr/bin/batctl from AUR/apt), pkexec would silently fail because
  the policy exec.path didn't match
- Detect CPU architecture (x86_64/arm64) and fail fast with a clear message
  on unsupported arches instead of a cryptic 404
- Check for required tools (curl, tar, glib-compile-schemas) before starting
- Auto-enable the extension via gnome-extensions if available, with fallback
  instructions if not
- Add logout reminder and UPower conflict hint to completion output
- Use trap for temp dir cleanup

commit.message:
Add free-running baud_ref_tick signal to UART for waveform debugging

Adds a continuously pulsing reference tick at the baud rate, independent
of TX/RX FSM state, useful as a timing reference in GTKWave.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: opensoc
repository.description: Open SoC based on Ibex RISC-V core

commit.message:
Replace minimal dual-UART test with bidirectional framed protocol

Adds a shared protocol header (uart_protocol.h) with SYNC/SEQ/LEN/PAYLOAD/CHECKSUM
framing. SoC0 (initiator) and SoC1 (responder) now perform a 3-phase test:
handshake with PING/PONG, 8 lockstep data rounds with integrity verification,
and DONE exchange with PASS/FAIL reporting. 16-byte payloads stress the 8-deep
UART FIFOs.
